channel.title: +Articles on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Mathur A temple IdgAh
item.description:
Source: TW

Significance

Is Mathura’s Shahi Idgah also on top of a destroyed temple? The current temple is very close to it. And the garbha griha is towards the mosque.

Not just any temple, but the site one of the greatest Hindu temples in antiquity.
It was much larger than any extant Gupta-era temples in North India today (Daśāvatāra temple in Deogarh, Nācnā-Kuṭhārā temple complex [Caturmukhanātha, etc.], Bhītargāon temple in Kanpur, Lakṣmaṇa temple in Sirpur, etc.). Unfortunately, like most significant North Indian temples (Bhojeśvara, Mārtaṇḍa, Mūlasthāna, Somanātha, etc.), it too was destroyed in the first few centuries of Islamic conquest.

item.title: Sujanagar mosque inscription
item.description:
विस्तारः (द्रष्टुं नोद्यम्)

Pratna Samiksha
A Journal of Archaeology

New Series
Volume 10
2019

Centre for Archaeological Studies & Training
Eastern India • Kolkata

Pratna Samiksha, New Series
is an annual, peer-reviewed, and international journal published by the Centre for Archaeological Studies & Training, Eastern India, Kolkata.

Editorial Address

Centre for Archaeological Studies & Training, Eastern India,

1 Satyen Roy Road, Behala, Kolkata 700 034
